proposal.reasoning: Lower activation threshold captures more upside before trailing kicks in, improving win rate on volatile micro-caps that spike quickly then retrace.
Increase micro-cap hard SL floor from 8% to 10% to reduce noise-induced stops on volatile tokens while maintaining the 3.0 ATR multiplier protection.
Micro-cap volatility spikes require slightly wider trailing to reduce whipsaws while maintaining profit capture, moving from 2.5 toward the 3.0+ range where trailing becomes more stable.

Current best (2.8) has high avgPnl (0.45%) but winRate slightly lower than 2.5 variant; incremental increase to 2.9 should widen trail just enough to reduce noise stops while preserving the 54-55% winRate sweet spot on volatile micro-caps.
Current best (2.8) shows diminishing returns vs 2.9; testing 2.7 balances tighter trailing to capture volatility swings on micro-caps without over-stopping on noise.

Current best uses 10% floor but micro-cap volatility suggests slightly tighter 9% minimum could improve Sharpe ratio without sacrificing win rate, as recent 2.8-2.9 atrMult tests show diminishing returns.

Current best (6.18) uses 10%; micro-cap volatility suggests a slightly higher floor (11%) may reduce whipsaw stops while maintaining downside protection, bridging the gap between tested 10% and 9% configurations.
Lower activation threshold allows trailing stop to engage sooner on micro-cap volatility, capturing more upside before stops trigger on noise while maintaining the sharp 6.18 score foundation.

Current best allows 15% max loss; increasing to 16% gives micro-cap volatility slightly more room while maintaining hard SL discipline, addressing the noise-sensitivity issue without abandoning stops like wider atrMult would.
Current 2.7 is optimal but slightly tighter at 2.6 may reduce noise-driven stops on micro-caps while maintaining the 55% win rate that drives the 6.18 score.

Current best params show atrMult=2.7 is slightly tight for micro-cap volatility; incrementally wider trail to 2.8 should reduce whipsaw stops while maintaining profitability based on the 55% win rate stability across recent tests.

Recent test with hardSlMaxPct=16 scored 6.17 (near-best), suggesting the ceiling can absorb larger swings on micro-caps; incrementally increasing to 17 may capture more volatile moves while maintaining Sharpe discipline.

Recent tests show hardSlMaxPct=16 scored 6.17 (nearly matching best), suggesting the optimal loss cap sits between 15-16; testing 16 as middle ground balances catastrophic loss prevention with trailing flexibility.
Recent tests show hardSlMaxPct=16 consistently matches best score (6.17) with stable 55% win rate, suggesting this is the optimal hard stop cap for micro-cap volatility.
Lower activation threshold captures more of the early momentum phase where micro-caps tend to have sharper moves, potentially improving win rate and average PnL without exposing to excessive noise.
